participant_id.Description: The subject ID of the participant

mfq1_care.Description: Response to MFQ–Whether or not someone suffered emotionally.

mfq2_fairness.Description: Response to MFQ–Whether or not some people were treated differently than others.

mfq3_loyalty.Description: Response to MFQ–Whether or not someone’s action showed love for his or her country.

mfq4_authority.Description: Response to MFQ-Whether or not someone showed a lack of respect for authority.

mfq5_sanctity.Description: Response to MFQ-Whether or not someone violated standards of purity and decency.

mfq6_catch.Description: Response to MFQ-Whether or not someone was good at math.

mfq7_care.Description: Response to MFQ-Whether or not someone cared for someone weak or vulnerable.

mfq8_fairness.Description: Response to MFQ-Whether or not someone acted unfairly.

mfq9_loyalty.Description: Response to MFQ-Whether or not someone did something to betray his or her group.

mfq10_authority.Description: Response to MFQ-Whether or not someone conformed to the traditions of society.

mfq11_sanctity.Description: Response to MFQ-Whether or not someone did something disgusting.

mfq12_care.Description: Response to MFQ-Whether or not someone was cruel.

mfq13_fairness.Description: Response to MFQ-Whether or not someone was denied his or her rights.

mfq14_loyalty.Description: Response to MFQ-Whether or not someone showed a lack of loyalty.

mfq15_authority.Description: Response to MFQ-Whether or not an action caused chaos or disorder.

mfq16_sanctity.Description: Response to MFQ-Whether or not someone acted in a way that God would approve of.

mfq17_care.Description: Response to MFQ-Compassion for those who are suffering is the most crucial virtue.

mfq18_fairness.Description: Response to MFQ-When the government makes laws, the number one principle should be ensuring that everyone is treated fairly.

mfq19_loyalty.Description: Response to MFQ-I am proud of my country’s history.

mfq20_authority.Description: Response to MFQ-Respect for authority is something all children need to learn.

mfq21_sanctity.Description: Response to MFQ-People should not do things that are disgusting, even if no one is harmed.

mfq22_catch.Description: Response to MFQ-It is better to do good than to do bad.

mfq23_care.Description: Response to MFQ-One of the worst things a person could do is hurt a defenseless animal.

mfq24_fairness.Description: Response to MFQ-Justice is the most important requirement for a society.

mfq25_loyalty.Description: Response to MFQ-People should be loyal to their family members, even when they have done something wrong.

mfq26_authority.Description: Response to MFQ-Men and women each have different roles to play in society.

mfq27_sanctity.Description: Response to MFQ-I would call some acts wrong on the grounds that they are unnatural.

mfq28_care.Description: Response to MFQ-It can never be right to kill a human being.

mfq29_fairness.Description: Response to MFQ-I think it’s morally wrong that rich children inherit a lot of money while poor children inherit nothing.

mfq30_loyalty.Description: Response to MFQ-It is more important to be a team player than to express oneself.

mfq31_authority.Description: Response to MFQ-If I were a soldier and disagreed with my commanding officer’s orders, I would obey anyway because that is my duty.

mfq32_sanctity.Description: Response to MFQ-Chastity is an important and valuable virtue.

mfq_care_avg.Description: Average score for the care/harm foundation in the Moral Foundations Questionnaire (MFQ).

mfq_fairness_avg.Description: Average score for the fairness/cheating foundation in the MFQ.

mfq_loyalty_avg.Description: Average score for the loyalty/betrayal foundation in the MFQ.

mfq_authority_avg.Description: Average score for the authority/subversion foundation in the MFQ.

mfq_sanctity_avg.Description: Average score for the sanctity/degradation foundation in the MFQ.

disgust1_monkey_meat.Description: Response to DS-R: I might be willing to try eating monkey meat, under some circumstances.

disgust2_human_hand.Description: Response to DS-R: It would bother me to be in a science class, and to see a human hand preserved in a jar.

disgust3_clear_throat.Description: Response to DS-R: It bothers me to hear someone clear a throat full of mucous.

disgust4_toilet_seat.Description: Response to DS-R: I never let any part of my body touch the toilet seat in public restrooms.

disgust5_avoid_graveyard.Description: Response to DS-R: I would go out of my way to avoid walking through a graveyard.

disgust6_cockroach_other.Description: Response to DS-R: Seeing a cockroach in someone else's house doesn't bother me.

disgust7_dead_body.Description: Response to DS-R: It would bother me tremendously to touch a dead body.

disgust8_seeing_vomit.Description: Response to DS-R: If I see someone vomit, it makes me sick to my stomach.

disgust9_sick_cook.Description: Response to DS-R: I probably would not go to my favorite restaurant if I found out that the cook had a cold.

disgust10_glass_eye.Description: Response to DS-R: It would not upset me at all to watch a person with a glass eye take the eye out of the socket.

disgust11_park_rat.Description: Response to DS-R: It would bother me to see a rat run across my path in a park.

disgust12_eat_paper.Description: Response to DS-R: I would rather eat a piece of fruit than a piece of paper.

disgust13_flyswatter_soup.Description: Response to DS-R: Even if I was hungry, I would not drink a bowl of my favorite soup if it had been stirred by a used but thoroughly washed flyswatter.

disgust14_heart_attack.Description: Response to DS-R: It would bother me to sleep in a nice hotel room if I knew that a man had died of a heart attack in that room the night before.

disgust15_maggots.Description: Response to DS-R: You see maggots on a piece of meat in an outdoor garbage pail.

disgust16_apple.Description: Response to DS-R: You see a person eating an apple with a knife and fork.

disgust17_urine.Description: Response to DS-R: While you are walking through a tunnel under a railroad track, you smell urine.

disgust18_soda.Description: Response to DS-R: You take a sip of soda, and then realize that you drank from the glass that an acquaintance of yours had been drinking from.

disgust19_dead_cat.Description: Response to DS-R: Your friend's pet cat dies, and you have to pick up the dead body with your bare hands.

disgust20_ketchup.Description: Response to DS-R: You see someone put ketchup on vanilla ice cream, and eat it.

disgust21_intestines.Description: Response to DS-R: You see a man with his intestines exposed after an accident.

disgust22_underwear.Description: Response to DS-R: You discover that a friend of yours changes underwear only once a week.

disgust23_chocolate.Description: Response to DS-R: A friend offers you a piece of chocolate shaped like dog doo.

disgust24_ashes.Description: Response to DS-R: You accidentally touch the ashes of a person who has been cremated.

disgust25_spoiled_milk.Description: Response to DS-R: You are about to drink a glass of milk when you smell that it is spoiled.

disgust26_condom.Description: Response to DS-R: As part of a sex education class, you are required to inflate a new unlubricated condom, using your mouth.

disgust27_earthworm.Description: Response to DS-R: You are walking barefoot on concrete, and you step on an earthworm.

secs3_security.Description: Response to SECS: Military and national security. (S).

secs5_welfare.Description: Response to SECS: Welfare benefits (reverse scored). (E).

secs11_family_unit.Description: Response to SECS: The family unit. (S)

iri1_fantasy.Description: Response to IRI-I daydream and fantasize, with some regularity, about things that might happen to me.

iri2_concern.Description: Response to IRI-I often have tender, concerned feelings for people less fortunate than me.

iri3_perspective.Description: Response to IRI-I sometimes find it difficult to see things from the 'other guy's' point of view.

iri4_concern.Description: Response to IRI-Sometimes I don't feel very sorry for other people when they are having problems.

iri5_fantasy.Description: Response to IRI-I really get involved with the feelings of the characters in a novel.

iri6_distress.Description: Response to IRI-In emergency situations, I feel apprehensive and ill-at-ease.

iri7_fantasy.Description: Response to IRI-I am usually objective when I watch a movie or play, and I don't often get completely caught up in it.

iri8_perspective.Description: Response to IRI-I try to look at everybody's side of a disagreement before I make a decision.

iri9_concern.Description: Response to IRI-When I see someone being taken advantage of, I feel kind of protective towards them.

iri10_distress.Description: Response to IRI-I sometimes feel helpless when I am in the middle of a very emotional situation.

iri11_perspective.Description: Response to IRI-I sometimes try to understand my friends better by imagining how things look from their perspective.

iri12_fantasy.Description: Response to IRI-Becoming extremely involved in a good book or movie is somewhat rare for me.

iri13_distress.Description: Response to IRI-When I see someone get hurt, I tend to remain calm.

iri14_concern.Description: Response to IRI-Other people's misfortunes do not usually disturb me a great deal.

iri15_perspective.Description: Response to IRI-If I'm sure I'm right about something, I don't waste much time listening to other people's arguments.

iri16_fantasy.Description: Response to IRI-After seeing a play or movie, I have felt as though I were one of the characters.

iri17_distress.Description: Response to IRI-Being in a tense emotional situation scares me.

iri18_concern.Description: Response to IRI-When I see someone being treated unfairly, I sometimes don't feel very much pity for them.

iri19_distress.Description: Response to IRI-I am usually pretty effective in dealing with emergencies.

iri20_concern.Description: Response to IRI-I am often quite touched by things that I see happen.

iri21_perspective.Description: Response to IRI-I believe that there are two sides to every question and try to look at them both.

iri22_concern.Description: Response to IRI-I would describe myself as a pretty soft-hearted person.

iri23_fantasy.Description: Response to IRI-When I watch a good movie, I can very easily put myself in the place of a leading character.

iri24_distress.Description: Response to IRI-I tend to lose control during emergencies.

iri25_perspective.Description: Response to IRI-When I'm upset at someone, I usually try to 'put myself in his shoes' for a while.

iri26_fantasy.Description: Response to IRI-When I am reading an interesting story or novel, I imagine how I would feel if the events in the story were happening to me.

iri27_distress.Description: Response to IRI-When I see someone who badly needs help in an emergency, I go to pieces.

iri28_perspective.Description: Response to IRI-Before criticizing somebody, I try to imagine how I would feel if I were in their place.

iri_fantasy_avg.Description: Average score for the Fantasy subscale of the Interpersonal Reactivity Index (IRI).

iri_perspective_avg.Description: Average score for the Perspective Taking subscale of the IRI.

iri_concern_avg.Description: Average score for the Empathic Concern subscale of the IRI.

iri_distress_avg.Description: Average score for the Empathic Distress subscale of the IRI.
